festivals.Name: Diwali
festivals.Significance: Festival of Lights; celebrates the victory of light over darkness and the return of Lord Rama to Ayodhya

festivals.Name: Holi
festivals.Significance: Festival of Colours; celebrates the arrival of spring and the victory of good over evil

festivals.Name: Eid-ul-Fitr
festivals.Significance: Marks the end of the holy fasting month of Ramadan

festivals.Name: Ganesh Chaturthi
festivals.Significance: Celebrates the birth of Lord Ganesha, the remover of obstacles

festivals.Name: Navratri / Durga Puja
festivals.Significance: Nine nights dedicated to the Goddess Durga; celebrates her victory over the demon Mahishasura

festivals.Name: Christmas
festivals.Significance: Celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ

festivals.Name: Raksha Bandhan
festivals.Significance: Celebrates the sacred bond of protection between brothers and sisters

festivals.Name: Republic Day
festivals.Significance: Commemorates the adoption of the Indian Constitution

festivals.Name: Independence Day
festivals.Significance: Commemorates freedom from British rule

festivals.Name: Gandhi Jayanti
festivals.Significance: Birthday of Mahatma Gandhi, the Father of the Nation

festivals.Name: Lohri
festivals.Significance: Winter solstice and harvest festival involving bonfires

festivals.Name: Baisakhi
festivals.Significance: Harvest festival and the foundation day of the Khalsa

festivals.Name: Karwa Chauth
festivals.Significance: Married women fast for the long life and well-being of their husbands

festivals.Name: Chhath Puja
festivals.Significance: Dedicated to the Sun God (Surya) and Chhathi Maiya

festivals.Name: Pongal
festivals.Significance: Harvest festival thanking the Sun God and cattle

festivals.Name: Onam
festivals.Significance: Celebrates the homecoming of the mythical King Mahabali

festivals.Name: Ugadi
festivals.Significance: Traditional New Year for Kannada, Telugu, and Marathi people

festivals.Name: Gudi Padwa
festivals.Significance: Marathi New Year; involves hoisting a decorated pole (Gudi)

festivals.Name: Vishu
festivals.Significance: Astronomical New Year

festivals.Name: Bihu
festivals.Significance: Celebrates different stages of the agricultural cycle

festivals.Name: Hornbill Festival
festivals.Significance: Promotes inter-tribal harmony and culture

festivals.Name: Hemis Festival
festivals.Significance: Celebrates the birth of Guru Padmasambhava

festivals.Name: Rath Yatra
festivals.Significance: Grand chariot procession of Lord Jagannath

festivals.Name: Pushkar Camel Fair
festivals.Significance: Livestock trade fair combined with religious bathing
